content:
# Matthew Chapter 15 — The EMET Bible (Literal)
**Translator:** EMET | **Source:** Codex Sinaiticus (א), 4th century CE | **Source Image:** source-texts/greek-NT/MAT_015_page_001.jpg, MAT_015_page_002.jpg

1. Then come to the Yeshua from Hierosolyma Pharisaioi and scribes, saying,

2. "Why do the disciples of you transgress the tradition of the elders? For they do not wash the hands of them when they eat bread."

3. And answering, he said to them, "Why do you also transgress the commandment of the God on account of the tradition of you?

4. For the God said, 'Honor the father and the mother,' and 'The [one] speaking-evil-of father or mother, let him end in death.'

5. But you say, 'Whoever says to the father or the mother, "A gift [is] whatever you might have been profited from me,"

6. he will not honor the father of him.' And you annulled the word of the God on account of the tradition of you.

7. Hypocrites! Well did Esaias prophesy concerning you, saying,

8. 'This people honors me with the lips, but the heart of them is far distant from me.

9. And in vain they worship me, teaching teachings [that are] commandments of men.'"

10. And having summoned the crowd, he said to them, "Hear and understand:

11. Not what enters into the mouth defiles the man, but what comes out of the mouth, this defiles the man."

12. Then having come, the disciples say to him, "Do you know that the Pharisaioi, having heard the word, were scandalized?"

13. And answering, he said, "Every plant which the Father of me, the heavenly, did not plant will be uprooted.

14. Leave them; they are blind guides of [the] blind. And if [a] blind [one] leads [a] blind [one], both will fall into a pit."

15. And answering, the Petros said to him, "Explain to us the parable."

16. And he said, "Are you also still without understanding?

17. Do you not understand that everything entering into the mouth goes into the belly and is cast out into [the] drain?

18. But the things coming out of the mouth come out of the heart, and those defile the man.

19. For out of the heart come evil thoughts, murders, adulteries, sexual-immoralities, thefts, false-testimonies, blasphemies.

20. These are the [things] defiling the man; but to eat with unwashed hands does not defile the man."

21. And having gone out from there, the Yeshua withdrew into the regions of Tyre and Sidon.

22. And behold, a Canaanite woman from those borders having come out was crying out, saying, "Have mercy on me, Lord, Son of David; the daughter of me is badly demonized."

23. But he did not answer her a word. And having come, the disciples of him were asking him, saying, "Dismiss her, for she cries out after us."

24. And answering, he said, "I was not sent except to the sheep, the lost [ones], of [the] house of Israel."

25. But she, having come, was worshiping him, saying, "Lord, help me."

26. And answering, he said, "It is not good to take the bread of the children and throw [it] to the dogs."

27. And she said, "Yes, Lord; for even the dogs eat from the crumbs falling from the table of the lords of them."

28. Then answering, the Yeshua said to her, "O woman, great [is] the faith of you; let it be done to you as you wish." And the daughter of her was healed from that hour.

29. And having departed from there, the Yeshua came alongside the sea of the Galilaia, and having gone up into the mountain, he was sitting there.

30. And came to him crowds great, having with themselves lame, blind, crippled, mute, and many others, and they laid them at the feet of him, and he healed them,

31. so that the crowd marveled, seeing mute [ones] speaking, crippled [ones] healthy, and lame [ones] walking, and blind [ones] seeing; and they glorified the God of Israel.

32. And the Yeshua, having summoned the disciples of him, said, "I am moved-with-compassion upon the crowd, for already three days they remain with me and do not have what they might eat; and to dismiss them fasting I do not wish, lest they faint on the road."

33. And the disciples say to him, "From where [do we get] in a wilderness so many loaves as to satisfy so great a crowd?"

34. And the Yeshua says to them, "How many loaves do you have?" And they said, "Seven, and a few small fish."

35. And having commanded the crowd to recline upon the ground,

36. he took the seven loaves and the fish, and having given thanks, he broke [them] and was giving to the disciples, and the disciples to the crowds.

37. And they all ate and were satisfied. And the excess of the fragments they took up, seven baskets full.

38. And the [ones] eating were four thousand men, apart from women and children.

39. And having dismissed the crowds, he embarked into the boat and came into the borders of Magadan.

---
## EMET's Textual Notes

1. **Χαναναία (v.22):** "Canaanite" — Matthew uses the archaic OT term; Mark's parallel (7:26) calls her "Syrophoenician." Matthew's choice evokes the ancient Canaanite-Israelite tension, making the woman's faith more remarkable.

2. **κυνάρια (v.26):** "Dogs" — diminutive form (κυνάρια, "little dogs/puppies"), not the harsh κύνες ("dogs"). The diminutive softens the metaphor slightly — these are household pets, not street scavengers.

3. **v.24 — "I was not sent except...":** Jesus states a mission limitation parallel to 10:5-6. The Canaanite woman's persistence breaks through this boundary, anticipating the universal mission of 28:19.

4. **Μαγαδάν (v.39):** Sinaiticus reads "Magadan" — other manuscripts read "Magdala" or "Magadan." The exact location is uncertain but is on the western shore of the Sea of Galilee.

— EMET, AI Translator
